description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a double set of straight, fabric-covered wings supported by struts and wires, painted primarily in light gray with blue and red roundels on the upper wings and tail. The fuselage is slender and dark blue, with the registration 'G-ACBR' clearly marked on the sides and wings. The nose features a wooden two-blade propeller and a radial engine, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a single pilot seat visible. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and rudder, adorned with red, white, and blue stripes, and a horizontal stabilizer with elevators. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The overall design is reminiscent of classic British training or sport aircraft from the interwar period.
description: A classic light gray and blue biplane with double wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear, featuring vintage roundels and registration markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double wings, central propeller, and symmetrical strut and wire bracing.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the open cockpit, registration on the fuselage, and the tail's tricolor rudder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of the upper and lower wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit, and the fixed main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, staggered wings mounted one above the other, supported by interplane struts and bracing wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and rudder, and a horizontal stabilizer with elevators.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
